article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: The summary largely attributes the article’s loaded language to the Department of Education and uses quotation marks, avoiding adopting the rhetoric as fact. One mildly value-laden term (“appropriately removing”) is framed as the department’s statement. Perspectives are mostly proportional to the source’s one-sided framing.

custom_reasoning.spin: Language is mostly neutral and uses attributive verbs (“characterized,” “said,” “called”); only “appropriately removing” carries slight value but is attributed.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All contested claims are presented as the department’s characterizations with quotes or attribution.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative statements are attributed; minimal risk from “appropriately” is tied to DOE’s stance.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No alarmist or emotionally charged language beyond quoted source terms.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasis on DOE perspective mirrors the source’s focus; no opposing side presented in the article to balance.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: No major perspectives present in the source are omitted; AAF mention is peripheral.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are factual; loaded terms appear only in quotes.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically charged terms like “Book Ban Hoax” are quoted and attributed.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone mirrors the source without amplifying negatives.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official statements as sources without dismissing others; aligns with source content.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Significantly more neutral than the source; relies on attribution and quotation rather than adopting rhetoric.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers DOE statements and case details; does not elaborate on AAF headline mention, which is minor, leading to slight underrepresentation.
